 FAIR OAKS RECREATION AND PARK DISTRICT BOARD OF
                                        DIRECTORS
 ONE THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED FORTY-FOURTH BOARD OF DIRECTORS’
                      REGULAR MEETING
                   Agenda for May 21, 2025
The regular meeting of the Board of Directors on May 21, 2025 will be conducted in person and via
                                                Zoom:


                           https://us02web.zoom.us/j/87345380207
                         US: +1 669 900 9128 Meeting ID: 873 453 80207

                              FAIR OAKS WATER DISTRICT
                  10326 FAIR OAKS BLVD. FAIR OAKS, CA 95628
                                May 21, 2025
                                  6:00 P.M.

   1. CALL TO ORDER

   2. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE

   3. ROLL CALL

   4. PRESENTATIONS/PROCLAMATIONS/RECOGNITION

     4.I. Presentation Of New Staff: Marketing Coordinator And Facility Tech Supervisor.

   5. AUDIENCE/PUBLIC COMMENT (NON-AGENDA ITEMS)
       (Any person may address the Board upon any subject within the jurisdiction
       of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park Distict. Each speaker is limited to a
       maximum of THREE (3) minutes. Any matter requiring Board action will be
       referred to staff or committee for a report and action at a subsequent
       meeting).

   6. CONSENT CALENDAR
       (All matters listed under Consent Calendar are considered by the Board of Directors to be
       routine and will be acted upon in a single motion. There will not be separate discussions
       of these items unless a request is made prior to the time the Board considers a motion to
       approve).

I, Michael J. Aho, District Administrator of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District, do
hereby certify that this agenda has been posted at 10326 Fair Oaks Blvd, Fair Oaks, California
and 4150 Temescal Street, Fair Oaks, California at least 72 hours prior to the meeting of the
Board of Directors in accordance with Government Code Section 54950.5, the Ralph M. Brown
Act.


If you need a disability-related accommodation to participate in these meetings, please
contact the Park District Office at (916) 966-1036 (voice) or (916) 966-9863 (fax).


To watch the meeting live on Facebook, please
visit: https://www.facebook.com/FORPD/live_videos

 Note: Regarding Sick-Leave hours, a Regular Full-Time employee may choose the Sick Leave payout option and utilize a maximum of
   320 hours (as identified within the table above) which will be compensated at 50% of the employees current hourly rate of pay.
However, upon retirement from the District, a Regular Full-Time employee December choose to utilize their accrued Sick Leave toward
CalPERS service credit with no maximum limit. Employees whose Sick Leave hours exceed 320 hours are noted but the hours in excess
of 320 not calculated as a liability in the table above. A Regular Part-Time employee will be compensated for a maximum of 160 hours
                                    of sick leave at 50% of the employees rate of pay upon termination.

                                                                      Quotation #2404113


Re: Village Park Community Center – Fair Oaks, CA                              Accepted: ______________

Dear Gage:                                                                     Date: ______________

We are pleased to provide you with this Materials Only quotation for our FM-200 Insulated Foam
Panels for the referenced job (per your docs.) of approximately 1,138 SqFt – 32 Panels

Base price for the bid (no specification) is a lump sum of:                               $31,792.00

FM-200 is constructed of:
   • .040” aluminum face (3003) smooth
   • .040” aluminum liner (3003) smooth
   • 1.5” Polyisocyanurate foam core (2.0lb density)
   • Faces - 1 Protean standard 2-caot 70% KYNAR “Medium Bronze”
   • Liners – standard primer or random KYNAR

The following items are also included in the base price:                                        Included
    • Fasteners
    • Flashings as shown

Freight to the Job Site (1 partial shipment):                                                   $2,200.00
Estimated Freight, actual price will be determined at time of shipping, job site
Delivery requires a flatbed truck with a tarped load. Partial loads and added shipments
will incur additional freight charges.




Shop Drawings:                                                                                  Included

Warranties                                                                                      Included
   • 1 Year manufacturer’s Warranty
   • 20 year finish warranty



Total price with freight:                                                                       $33,992.00
                                                                                          Sales Tax 2,702.32
                                                                                             Total 36,694.32




                         Protean Construction Products | Architectural Metal Panels
                 11901 Riverwood Drive, Burnsville, MN 55337 | 952-895-4000 | www.protean.com
                                           Inclusions and Assumptions
Delivery: Lead time for a job of this nature will typically run (8 – 10 weeks) for first delivery from receipt of
approved color, approved shop drawings, and field verified or guaranteed dimensions. Actual lead time will vary
based upon customer requested delivery and work load in our facility. Protean will appoint a dedicated Project
Manager to this job as a liaison between all parties, to help facilitate desired job site delivery and to keep you
appraised of the schedule for your order.




Shop Drawings: Protean shall provide a complete set of shop drawings for your approval. Shop drawings will
include a cover sheet, plans, elevations, section details and item details, as necessary. For a job of this complexity
(2 – 4) weeks are typically required for drawings to be submitted; actual lead time will vary based upon projects
Protean has in process. Pricing assumes two submittals of shop drawings and product data. Protean will charge
for additional submittals due to changes in scope, Changes in original design intent changes made to contract
drawings, design phase alterations, customer requests and errors or omissions by the customer or other (but
exclusive of errors and omissions by Protean).

Releases: This quotation assumes 1 phase of release. Please let us know if more releases are anticipated, as
additional releases will result in price increases.

Field Verified Construction: Custom fabricated panels and systems from Protean are 100% ready for field
installation. Our pricing assumes that the customer will provide correct and accurate field verification. Field
verification must be provided on Protean’s submittal drawings and be based upon face dimensions of the panels or
the existing condition of the sheeting or sub-framing measured at a point where our panels are ready to be
attached. Failure to provide field verification as described above and/or that result in dimensional changes will
result in price increases. Contact Protean if you have any questions or would like assistance. Protean is willing to
assist on site with field verification for a fee.

Changes in Scope: Changes in scope are expected and understood on complex projects. Protean will strive to
meet your original schedule despite changes in scope. Change orders due to scope changes will be processed and
provided on a time, materials (and if applicable) change in freight basis.

Color Match: In situations where color matching is critical, Protean encourages the use of fluoropolymer paint for
best color matching and color consistency. When paint is not an acceptable alternate, Protean will anodize grade
material when an anodized finish is specified. NOTE: Anodized colors and finishes will vary from one lot of metal
material to the next and takes on the surface characteristics of the base metal. With the inherent inconsistencies
of anodizing Protean cannot guaranty a) the uniformity of the finish coloring on our products, including
discoloration at weld lines or b) an indiscernible color match to extruded parts (windows, doors, etc.) or existing
anodized materials Protean will be unable to honor requests for remakes of anodized products based solely upon
color match or color consistency.




                          Protean Construction Products | Architectural Metal Panels
                  11901 Riverwood Drive, Burnsville, MN 55337 | 952-895-4000 | www.protean.com
                                         Exclusions and Assumptions

Items excluded from this quotation:

    •    Fabricated sample panels for submittal with a specified finish color or non-standard material.
    •    ny & all flashing, trim or other sheet metal accessories not specifically included.
    •    Sealants, backer rod and weeps except as noted in inclusions.
    •    Support framing and subgirts
    •    Penetrations, vapor barrier, or related materials except as noted in inclusions.
    •    Field labor and verification, measurements, pre-installation meetings, site visits or supervision.
    •    Any loading and unloading of materials. Customer to supply adequate equipment at delivery to
         unload the shipment.
    •    Job specific engineering or structural calculations
    •    Job specific testing
    •    Mock ups.
    •    Sales tax, use tax and bonds.
    •    Any and all materials or services not specifically noted herein are expressly excluded from the
         scope of this proposal.

Thank you for this opportunity to serve you.

Sincerely,
Michael Henry
Michael Henry
Senior Estimator




        Due to current volatility in the metal markets, pricing has to be confirmed at time of order.

                TERMS OF SALE: 30% Down, Progress Payments through course of project.
                                 Cancellation: 20% of Contract Balance
                 See attached General Terms and Conditions of Sale, Paragraphs 3 and 17.

(Down payment required to protect pricing from materials market changes. If the customer refuses down payment
  terms, material escalation charges may be applied as necessary. Freight is estimated. If the customer disagrees
   with the proposed freight charge, they may elect to arrange shipping and freight charges will not be included.)




                         Protean Construction Products | Architectural Metal Panels

                     MATERIAL ONLY --- GENERAL TERMS & CONDITIONS OF SALE

 1. DEFINITIONS - The word "Seller" as used herein means Protean Construction Products, Inc., and the word "Buyer" means
   the purchaser of material and services hereunder from the Seller.
 2. CREDIT APPROVAL - Acceptance of contracts is at all times subject to the Seller's credit approval and the Seller reserves
   the right to require full or partial payment in advance if, in the Seller's opinion, the financial condition of the Buyer does
   not justify shipment of material. If at any time, in the Seller's judgment, reasonable doubt exists as to the Buyer's financial
   responsibility, or if Buyer is past due in payment of any amount owing to Seller under this or any other contract, Seller
   reserves the right, without liability and without prejudice to any other remedies to suspend performance, decline to
   ship, or stop any material in transit, until Seller receives payment of all amounts owing to Seller whether or not due, or
   adequate assurance of such payment.
 3. TERMS OF PAYMENT – A down payment may be required to hold pricing. Terms are net cash thirty (30) days from the
   date of each invoice, including material progress billings. Each shipment will be invoiced when shipped, including Shop
   Drawings. Liquidated damages for late payment shall be charged at the rate of one- and one-half percent (1 1/2%) per
   month.
 4. SHIPPING TERMS - All pricing is based on materials F.O.B. shipping point with freight prepaid and added to invoice at
   actual, unless otherwise noted.
 5. LOSS OR DAMAGE IN TRANSIT - The Seller will not be responsible for loss or damage to material caused by transportation
   companies, nor will the Seller assume any obligation for filing a claim for the same.
 6. CLAIMS FOR SHORTAGES, DELIVERY OF IMPROPER MATERIAL OR DELIVERY OF DEFECTIVE MATERIAL - Buyer agrees to
   inspect the material upon delivery, and no claims for shortages or delivery of apparently improper or defective material
   will be accepted by Seller unless written notice is sent to Seller within ten (10) days from the day of delivery. Seller will
   in no event pay or be liable for any claim resulting from the installation or use by the Buyer of apparently improper
   material or of material having apparent defects or damage.
 7. SHOP DRAWINGS - Seller, if directed by the Buyer and included in the bid proposal, will prepare shop drawings for the
   Buyer's use in obtaining approval for Seller's materials and the specific job details. The Buyer will be responsible for
   verifying all dimensions for compliance with the contract documents and field verification of the structural support
   members prior to the material being released for fabrication. Replacement of materials because of dimensional
   discrepancies or field changes will be handled as set forth in paragraph 15 (Changes in the Work) of this agreement.
 8. MANUFACTURING SCHEDULE - Pricing is based on a continuous manufacturing schedule in the phases of release quoted.
   Should this process be interrupted through no fault of the Seller, such as lack of field dimensions, Seller shall be entitled
   to additional compensation. Any cost associated with such delays shall be identified by Seller and agreed to by Buyer
   prior to continuation with manufacturing.
 9. RETURNS - No returns will be accepted by Seller unless previously authorized in writing by an officer of the firm.
10. DELAYS - All orders are accepted subject to delays due to strikes, fire, flood, shortage of materials, delays in transit, force
   majeurs, government priority or other regulations or other cause beyond our control. We assume no responsibility for
   delays occasioned by default of payment terms on prior invoice.
11. PENALTIES AND BACKCHARGES - Seller shall in no event be liable for penalties or damages except as may be specifically
   provided for herein. Credit will not be allowed for any cost or expense Buyer may incur in replacing or correcting material
   delivered hereunder unless Buyer shall give Seller reasonable written notice before any such cost or expense is incurred
   and obtain Seller's prior written approval thereof.
12. RETAINAGE - It is agreed that no retainage is allowed on this sale for labor or materials and that invoices will be paid in
   full within the thirty-day period specified. Any legal fees or other collection costs shall be borne by Buyer.
13. The Seller shall not be liable for any loss or damage, including any special or consequential damages, due to delay
   resulting from any cause beyond the Seller's reasonable control, including, but not limited to, Acts of God, fires, the
   elements, accidents, strikes or other labor difficulties, wars, riots or national emergencies, embargoes, government acts,
   regulations or requests, priorities of any kind, delays by transportation companies, shortages of energy, fuel, supplies or
   material or delays in obtaining material from usual sources of supply.



14. TAXES - If the Seller is required to pay any sales, use, excise, personal property, or other similar tax or charge incident to




                         Protean Construction Products | Architectural Metal Panels
                 11901 Riverwood Drive, Burnsville, MN 55337 | 952-895-4000 | www.protean.com
   the performance of this contract, the Buyer shall reimburse the Seller therefore upon request unless such tax or charge
   is specifically included in the sale price. The foregoing shall not apply to any taxes, the prepayment or collection of which
   by Seller is excused by reason of delivery to Seller of a valid exemption certificate.
15. CHANGES IN THE WORK - Seller will proceed with changes in the work herein specified only upon receipt of written order
   from Buyer specifying the requested change and containing an agreed price and schedule adjustment. Seller's election
   to proceed without such prior written order in any instance shall not be deemed a waiver of Seller's right to receive price
   and schedule adjustments therefore or to insist upon such prior written orders for any subsequent changes in the work.
16. INSTALLATION START-UP - Seller, if desired by the buyer and included in the bid proposal or change order, may elect to
   place a manufacturer's representative on site for the early start up stage of the installation to aid the Buyer or installer
   in the use of our product. The representative will be available to answer questions relating to the product and standard
   application methods, but will not be responsible for the layout, supervision of the installers’ personnel, or to certify the
   acceptance of the installation.
17. CANCELLATIONS - This contract may be canceled or modified only by written agreement between the parties, except as
   elsewhere provided herein. Should this agreement be terminated, Buyer agrees to pay Seller for all work executed and
   loss sustained in regard to any material, labor, equipment, and tools, plus 20% of contract balance. Buyer's insistence
   upon canceling or suspending fabrication or shipment or Buyer's failure to furnish information when required, may be
   treated by Seller as a breach of contract by Buyer and Seller may cancel any unshipped balance without prejudice to any
   other remedies Seller may have.
18. LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY - Buyer's exclusive remedy against Seller for any damages suffered by Buyer in connection
   with the material and arising out of this transaction shall be for breach of contract, and Seller's liability in this regard shall
   be limited to repair or replacement of defective or nonconforming material or repayment of the purchase price paid
   therefore by Buyer, as Seller may in its sole discretion elect. Because the sales price herein makes no provision for such
   risk, Seller shall in no event be liable to Buyer for any special or consequential damages including, but not limited to,
   erection costs, lost profits, good will, loss of time, inconvenience or commercial loss. Any action for breach of this
   contract must be commenced within one (1) year after the cause of action shall occur, and no such action may be
   maintained which is not commenced within such period.
19. WARRANTY - Seller warrants that the material sold hereunder will conform to the description stated herein (subject to
   tolerances and variations consistent with current trade practices and practical testing and inspection methods); that
   Seller will convey good title thereto (except for any liens or encumbrances that may be caused by Buyer); and that Seller
   will repair or replace any defective or nonconforming material or repay the purchase price, as Seller may elect, if notified
   thereof in writing within one (1) year from the date of delivery. This is Seller's sole warranty with respect to the material.
   SELLER MAKES NO OTHER WARRANTY OF ANY KIND WHATEVER, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED AND ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES
   OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE WHICH EXCEED THE AFORESAID OBLIGATION ARE
   HEREBY DISCLAIMED BY SELLER AND EXCLUDED FROM THIS TRANSACTION.
20. ENTIRE AGREEMENT - The terms stated herein constitute the sole terms and conditions of sale between Buyer and Seller,
   all prior representations and understandings having been merged herein. No other terms, conditions or understandings,
   whether oral or written, shall be binding upon Seller unless hereafter made in writing and signed by Seller's authorized
   representatives. By signing and returning this proposal, or by issuing a contract (in form of purchase order or otherwise)
   to Seller on the basis of this proposal, Buyer indicates its acceptance of the terms hereof and agrees to be bound by same
   in lieu of any different or additional terms proposed by Buyer or contained in Buyer's contract.

Quote #: 20291745 Revision 0                                                                                                                          Page | 1
General Notes:

    1.    All sales subject to the standard terms and conditions of All Weather Insulated Panels (AWIP).
    2.    Delivery dates contingent upon AWIP sourcing / acquiring raw materials. Customer milestones:
                a. Customer approval / return of key documents (sales order acknowledgement, shop drawings and color approvals)
                b. Special colors and gauges will increase lead time. Please inquire of AWIP for approximate lead time
    3.    AWIP will not assume responsibility for supplier delays outside of our control.
    4.    AWIP shall be held harmless for any and all liquidated damages for delays outside of our control.
    5.    AWIP panels will be made of either galvanized steel aluminum zinc coated steel face sheets depending on supplier availability at
          the time.
    6.    All required sealants (tube and tape sealants) to be field applied by installer unless specifically noted in this quote and shop
          drawings (additional charges apply for shop applied sealants).
    7.    Standard AWIP payment terms are net 30 days – with approved credit.
    8.    Package and Lump Sum quotes assume quantities are derived from most recent and complete bid drawings and all information
          has been provided to AWIP at time of quote. Special customer loading / shipping requests may increase quantity of estimated
          truckloads.
    9.    Usage of a PVDF finish on the liner side of panels requires that protective peel coat be applied during the production process to
          prevent marring of the coating. This peel coat will need to be removed in the field by the installing contractor.
Wall Panel Notes:
    10.   AdobeTexture™ colors (if applicable): Regal White, Sandstone, Surrey Beige, Pearl Gray.
    11.   All Wall panel trim to be same gauge and color as panel facings (unless otherwise noted).
    12.   Wall panels minimum length is 8’0”. Shorter panels require a short cut charge of $23.00 / panel.
    13.   Embossed facings are standard for AWIP exterior and interior wall panels. Non-Embossed panel facings may exhibit scalloping or
          other surface imperfections. A signed waiver is required on all non-embossed panels.
Roof Panel Notes:
    14. All roof trim to be the same gauge and color as panel facings (unless otherwise noted).
    15. SR-2 roof panel seamer rental – minimum 2-week rental. Credit card deposit required. Customer responsible for all shipping
        charges. Rental ends upon return to D.I. Roof Seamers. Seamer rental and operation information available at:
        www.awipseamers.com.
    16. Roof panel end lap cutbacks are factory cut and removed in the field. Factory removal charge is $20.00 / panel.
Ceiling Panel Notes:
    17. Ceiling hanger assembly does not include expanding foam sealant, vapor barrier tape, tie wire or connection to structure.
    18. Complete ceiling hanger assembly system depends on kit ordered:
             a. Kit #1 – AL-05 Aluminum Ceiling Tee (16’) and AL-02 Aluminum Splice Plates only
             b. Kit #2 – AL-05 Aluminum Ceiling Tee (16’), AL-02 Aluminum Splice Plates, ½” Clevis Assembly, 3/8” x 1-1/2” Hex Bolt, 3/8”
                 Washers, 3/8” Nuts, #12 – 14 x 1” Tek 3 Phillips Pancake Head Fasteners, PW-01 Plate Washers
                        i. Seismic Brace Plates are available for an additional fee and are not included in the base price for Kit #2.
                       ii. Kit #2 does not include expanding foam sealant, vapor barrier tape, threaded rod, tie wire or connection to
                           structure.
             c. Kit #3 – AL-05 Aluminum Ceiling Tee (16’), AL-02 Aluminum Splice Plates, ½” Clevis Assembly, 3/8” x 1-1/2” Hex Bolt, 3/8”
                 Washers, 3/8” Nuts, #12 – 14 x 1” Tek 3 Phillips Pancake Head Fasteners, PW-01 Plate Washers, JC-01 Joint Cover and
                 insulwrap (peel and seal) 4” width.
                        i. Kit #3 does not include expanding foam sealant, threaded rod, tie wire or connection to structure.
Exclusions:
    19.   All structural supports.
    20.   Concrete fasteners.
    21.   All field labor and installation costs.
    22.   Engineer’s stamp on shop drawings unless specifically noted.
    23.   Cut out trim for openings cut by other trades not shown on AWIP shop drawings.
    24.   All exposed sealants (silicone, urethane, pourable, etc.).
    25.   All thermal breaks (all panel thermal break cuts to be performed in the field).

Scope:
 This change order is based on the following:

 - (2) Approx. 6'-0" Single Line Ramp Rails at Amphitheater exit per 1/A1.3 & E/A1.4
 - (4) 3" Plastic Pipe Sleeves (FOB)

Urgency                  Normal



Activity

Question                  Question from Gage Burget Bobo Construction, Inc. on Tuesday, Jan 30, 2024 at 09:06 AM PST
                          We have received the following from R&D Electric:

                          Plan Sheet V2 E3.1 Note 7 shows the power requirements for the Front Sliding Door with a single point of connection.
                          The submittals received by R&D for the door being installed shows multiple junction boxes with conduit runs. Please
                          advise.

                          Attachments
                          RFI #095 V2 Sliding Door Power Requirements.pdf


                          Awaiting an Official Response

                    WMB Response | JR | 2024-03-19

                    All wiring inside the door noted by others was called to attention by AOR at submittal review for
                    coordination. It is the intent of the contract documents and coordination section of sliding hangar door
                    specification to require close coordination with associated trades for a complete installation of the
                    door system.

                    Regarding the single point of connection, please see attached EEOR memo, nxt pg. Manufacturer
                    has confirmed they require a connection for each door operator.




Bobo Construction Inc.                                                 Page 1 of 1                             Printed On: Jan 30, 2024 09:06 AM PST
                                                                                          HCS
                                                                                      Engineering

March 18th, 2024
Joel Rehbien
WMB Architects Inc.
5757 Pacific Ave, #226
Stockton, CA 95207

Re:       Fair Oaks Amphitheater
          RFI # 401 V2-Sliding Door Power Requirements

Dear Joel,

The following is our response to the questions in RFI # 401 V2-Sliding Door Power Requirements:

Subject: RFI # 401 V2-Sliding Door Power Requirements

Question asked by Gage Burget (Bobo Construction, Inc.)

Question:

We have received the following from R&D Electric:

Plan Sheet V2 E3.1 Note 7 shows the power requirements for the Front Sliding Door with a single point
of connection. This submittal received by R&D for the door being installed shows multiple junction
boxes with conduit runs. Please advise.

          Response: The Contractor is to provide a dedicated 120V 20Amp circuit to each side of the
          curtain for motor power. One is currently shown, provide the second per manufacturer
          requierments.0

Please don’t hesitate to call if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

HCS Engineering Inc.

Richard Smith, PE
Electrical Engineer, P.E.

Dear Joel,

The following is our response to the questions in RFI # 401 V2-Sliding Door Power Requirements:

Subject: RFI # 401 V2-Sliding Door Power Requirements

Question asked by Gage Burget (Bobo Construction, Inc.)

Question:

We have received the following from R&D Electric:

Plan Sheet V2 E3.1 Note 7 shows the power requirements for the Front Sliding Door with a single point
of connection. This submittal received by R&D for the door being installed shows multiple junction
boxes with conduit runs. Please advise.

          Response: The Contractor is to provide a dedicated 120V 20Amp circuit to each side of the
          curtain for motor power. One is currently shown, provide the second per manufacturer
          requierments.0

Please don’t hesitate to call if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

HCS Engineering Inc.

POTENTIAL CHANGE ORDER TITLE: RFI#404: V3 - Compromised Windows


CHANGE REASON: Client Request


POTENTIAL CHANGE ORDER DESCRIPTION: (The Contract Is Changed As Follows)
RFI#404: V3 - Compromised Windows
Remove windows designated #3, #4, # 7 ,#8, #9.
Remove exterior wood trim if present. Remove 6" +/- of existing stucco around window
opening as required for new stucco/paper /wire tie-in.
Infill wall with 2x DF wood framing, unfaced batt insulation and sheathing depth to match
existing. Install new 3 coat stucco system with moisture barrier paper/wire lath
overlapping existing and finish to match existing building texture.

Remove windows designated #1, #2, #5, #6
Remove 6" +/- of existing stucco around window opening as required for new stucco/paper
/wire tie-in.
Install new widows within existing opening. Window system and finish to match approved
Andersen Window E-Series submittal manufacturer/model and finish; match existing
mullion/sash heights and operations as existing. Provide manufacturer data and shop
drawings for review = field verify existing rough opening sizes.
Install new 3 coat stucco system with paper/wire lath overlapping existing and finish to
match existing building texture.

Window #10 is to be remain as is

                                                                                       CHANGE ORDER REQUEST
                                                                                                          9
                                                                                    Title: Patch repair at additional existing windows at Clubhouse
                                                                                    Project Name: VP23-0007_Village Park Renovation
                                                                                    Project Address: 7997 California Avenue
                                                                                                      Fair Oaks, CA 95628
                                                                                    COR Date: 06/01/2024
                                                                                    VPI - Lath Plaster Job Number: VP23-0007
                                                                                    Customer Job Number:
                                                                                    Customer Reference Number: CE#219-RFI#404 Compromised
                                                                                    Windows

       Our Information                                                              Customer Information

       VPI - Lath Plaster                                                           BOBO Construction
       2400 Alhambra Boulevard                                                      9722 Kent Street
       Sacramento, CA 95817                                                         Elk Grove, CA 95624
       Phone: (888)-701-5795

       Description of Change Order Request
       Windows have been compromised and must be replaced or infilled. There are 8-10 windows that requires us to demo stucco around window
       6" and do moisture barrier, and lath and plaster repair. Every attempt to waterproof correctly will be done but owner must understand
       existing vapor barrier is compromised and in some cases there is no paper to tie into to at these locations. The only way to ensure weather
       tightness is to pull all existing plaster off the building, which we know is not feasible. GC will cut plaster back 6" and we will chip out edge of
       plaster to expose wire and paper.

       Labor
           Description                                                  Qty (HR)         Unit (HR)                       Rate (HR)             Total Cost

           Foreman Lath-chipping plaster out at                                 8        ST                                 $116.02              $928.16
           windows
           Journeyman Lath-Chipping plaster                                     8        ST                                $109.28               $874.24
           out at windows
           Foreman Lath-Lath patch at windows                                   8        ST                                 $116.02              $928.16
           Journeyman Lath-Lath patch at                                       10        ST                                $109.28             $1,092.80
           windows
           Foreman Plaster-Scratch & Brown                                      8        ST                                $106.77               $854.16
           Journeyman Plaster-Cover, Scratch                                   16        ST                                $101.83             $1,629.28

Travis Nicholas

Re: Fair Oaks Village Park - Asbestos Abatement

Dear Travis:

West Coast Environmental proposes to remove and dispose of asbestos materials per job walk,
emails, Environmental Report, Local, State, and Federal Regulations.

Asbestos Scope of Work –

      Removal of asbestos wall mastic found inside the Village Park Facility on assumed white
      lead-based paint. All work will be performed under negative pressure using HEPA filtration
      equipment. Critical barriers will be placed over all doors, windows, HVAC, or other
      penetrations. Removal of <1% asbestos stucco 6” around on 9 windows. Work will be
      performed using regulated area and drop poly. All waste will be contained, manifested, and
      disposed of per regulations. We have included all required notifications and fees to
      Regulatory Entities.
                                             Interior Asbestos Bid = $4,500.00
     Exterior Asbestos Breakdown
        75 hrs @ $92.82/hr = $6,961.40      Exterior  Asbestos Bid = $9,700.00
                                                                      $9,691.40
 Material/Waste/Equipment = $1,465.80
                OH+P 15% = $1,264.10
                     Total = $9,691.40
This proposal is subject to the following terms and conditions:

       1) Power/Water services to be provided by owner.
       2) WCE, Inc. will provide a $5,000,000 Commercial Liability Insurance Policy.
       3) Bid is based on prevailing wages. DIR 1000002083.
       4) Removal of loose items in the work area are excluded.
       5) We exclude repair of surfaces due to containment attachment.

Should you have any questions regarding this proposal, please contact me at (916) 852-7200.
Thank you for the opportunity to bid on this project.

Sincerely,                                                                        3181 Fitzgerald Road
                                                                            Rancho Cordova, CA 95742

Urgency                  Normal



Activity

Question                  Question from Gage Burget Bobo Construction, Inc. on Thursday, Feb 8, 2024 at 02:18 PM PST
                          There are a total of 9 of the existing windows around the exterior of the Clubhouse that appear to have some dry rot damage
                          (see attached drawing markup and photos for locations). In previous meetings, the district has expressed potential concern
                          about these. Do these need to be addressed and if so how are we to proceed?

                          Attachments
                          RFI 404 - Compromised Windows.pdf


                          Awaiting an Official Response

                         Remove windows designated #3, #4, # 7 ,#8, #9.
                         Remove exterior wood trim if present. Remove 6" +/- of existing stucco around window
                         opening as required for new stucco/paper /wire tie-in.
                         Infill wall with 2x DF wood framing, unfaced batt insultion and sheathing depth to match
                         existing. Install new 3 coat stucco system with moisture barrier paper/wire lath
                         overlapping existing and finish to match existing building texture.

                         Remove windows designated #1, #2, #5, #6 , #10.
                         Remove 6" +/- of existing stucco around window opening as required for new stucco/paper
                         /wire tie-in.
                         Install new widows within existing opening. Window system and finish to match approved
                         Andersen Window E-Series submittal manufacturer/model and finish; match existing
                         mullion/sash heights and operations as existing. Provide manufacturer data and shop
                         drawings for review = field verify existing rough opening sizes.
                         Install new 3 coat stucco system with paper/wire lath overlapping existing and finish to
                         match existing building texture.

                          WMB Architects - 4-22-24

           STAFF REPORT

Meeting Date:        April 16, 2025

To:                  Board of Directors

From:                Michael J. Aho – District Administrator

Subject:             Construction Project Allowance Increase



I.    Recommendation
      Approve the increase in the Allowance for the contract with Bobo by
      $300,000 to ensure the project can be completed without financial
      interruption. This increased Allowance will be utilized for road paving and
      addressing unforeseen conditions that may be encountered as the project
      progresses. The funding for this increase will be sourced by reallocating
      $100,000 from the Amphitheater Seat Replacement budget surplus and
      $200,000 from the Cell Tower sale proceeds.
II.   Background
      The District's contract with Bobo is approaching its final stages, having
      successfully managed earlier phases that were prone to unpredictability.
      While considerable progress has been made, the project requires additional
      financial resources to address general site issues and unforeseen conditions
      specifically related to road paving. In Bobo’s contract the initial Allowance
      of $1.8 million has been fully allocated due to various unforeseen site
      conditions, weather-related delays, and escalations in material costs. To
      facilitate the completion of the remaining work without disruption, an
      increase in the Allowance is essential. It's important to note that the specific
      funding request for the road paving costs, amounting to $654,046, is being
      addressed under a separate item during this Board meeting.


      Originally, the plan was to fund these additional needs including the Road
      paving through a $1.4 million Senate Appropriation that was awarded for
      FY2025 (through Senator Padilla’s office). However, that federal funding
      was rescinded in March 2025 after the passage of the Full-Year Continuing
       Budget Resolution. In response, the District has reapplied for this funding
       for FY2026, with strong support from Senator Padilla, Senator Schiff, and
       Representative Bera. While we are optimistic about securing those funds
       next fiscal year, the project cannot wait; immediate action is needed to
       cover the shortfall and keep the project on track.


III.   Problem /Situation/ Request
       Allocating this additional $300,000 to the Bobo contract Allowance is being
       requested for the following reasons:
       • Completion of Critical Work: Road paving is a final critical
           component of the project that must be completed to meet project
           specifications and to be able to obtain an Occupancy Permit from
           Sacramento County. Additional change orders are anticipated for the
           overall project as we finalize construction work. Without increasing the
           Allowance, there is a risk that these necessary tasks could exceed the
           current budget, causing delays or requiring urgent action later.

       •   Reduced Risk of Overruns: Initially, the project's $1.8 million
           Allowance was completely allocated to manage a variety of unforeseen
           challenges, including unknown underground conditions, delays due to
           adverse weather, product price increases, product availability issues,
           and necessary design changes. The proposed addition of $300,000 to
           the Allowance is specifically designed to address any remaining
           unanticipated issues effectively. This proactive financial strategy is
           intended to mitigate the risk of potential overruns and eliminates the
           necessity for last-minute requests for additional funding from the
           Board.

       •   Maintaining Project Momentum: Approving the funding now
           ensures the contractor (Bobo) can proceed with final tasks without
           interruption. This avoids potential contractor
           demobilization/remobilization costs or schedule delays that could occur
           if funding uncertainty forces a slowdown.


IV.    Financial Analysis
       Staff have identified two internal funding sources to cover the $300,000
       Allowance increase without impacting the general fund or requiring new
       external funds:
       • Amphitheater Seat Replacement Project Budget: We propose
           reallocating $100,000 from the Amphitheater Seat Replacement capital
           project to support the increased Allowance. The original budget for this
           project was $906,934, which included an Allowance of $138,035. Given
           the significant progress made on this project, staff is confident that
           $100,000 can be redirected without jeopardizing its completion,
           leaving a balance of $38,035 in the project's Allowance.

       •   Cell Tower Sale Proceeds – Utilize $200,000 from the proceeds of
           the recent cell tower sale. The District currently holds $972,939 from
           this sale in a reserve account. Using $200,000 from this reserve will
           not adversely affect other projects. Importantly, this $200,000 is
           expected to be reimbursed once pending external funds are received:
           the District is awaiting a Prop 68 grant disbursement of $50,005 and
           the release of Federal House FY2022 Appropriation funds (earmarked
           via Rep. Bera’s office) of $560,929. Once these funds are received,
           they will replenish the cell tower proceeds account by the amount used
           for the Bobo contract.

       The combination of these two sources provides the necessary $300,000,
       ensuring the Bobo contract Allowance can be increased now to keep the
       project moving forward.

Respectfully Submitted,


Michael J. Aho
District Administrator

1. REVISION TO SHEET 4.3:
• Add concrete approximately 36 feet of retaining wall.
• Add approximately 20 square feet of Cobble Type 2

• Remove valve 17 and approximately 25 shrub bubblers

tree.

• Adjust ramp and pavement at electrical box locations.

• Adjust elevations at the ramp, and along the concrete wall, where
electrical equipment is located.

                                                                                              Bobo Village Park T&M Sheet revised page 1 of 1 - Printed 10/23/2024
                                  ASI #32-VOLUME 1

                      VILLAGE PARK RENOVATION PROJECT

                                  November 6th, 2024



The following changes, modifications, or additions to the drawings with this ASI are made a
part thereof and are subject to all of the requirements thereof as if originally specified.

CHANGES TO THE DRAWINGS

The following revision descriptions shall be included with the Village Park Renovation
project, Sheets 4.3, 5.3, 6.3, C1.3, and C2.3, dated November 5th, 2024.


      1. REVISION TO SHEET 4.3:
           • Add concrete approximately 36 feet of retaining wall.
           • Add approximately 20 square feet of Cobble Type 2
           • Adjust ramp and pavement at electrical box locations.
           • Remove approximately 38 square feet of pavement

      2. REVISIONS TO SHEET 5.3:
           • Remove valve 17 and approximately 25 shrub bubblers
           • Add vine bubblers
           • Adjust irrigation lateral lines and valve locations based on new
              improvements.

      3. REVISIONS TO SHEET 6.3:
           • Remove approximately 22, 1-gallon shrubs, 2 vines, and 16, 5-gallon
              shrubs
           • Add mulch-only area at the electrical equipment and under the existing
              tree.

      4. REVISIONS TO SHEET C1.3:
           • Adjust ramp and pavement at electrical box locations.

      5. REVISIONS TO SHEET C2.3
           • Adjust elevations at the ramp, and along the concrete wall, where
              electrical equipment is located.

Question                  Question from Gage Burget Bobo Construction, Inc. on Friday, Jun 21, 2024 at 12:24 PM PDT
                          We have received the following from R&D Electric:

                          Power Pedestals show 2 current carrying conductors and 1 ground (see attached drawings). During install we found we need
                          a 3rd current carrying as a neutral. Please advise how we are to proceed and advise on the cost impact as this was not
                          included in the bid set.

                          RFI #123 V1 - Power Pedestal Conductors.pdf


                          Awaiting an Official Response

 Information Request / Description:

Power Pedestal show 2 current carrying conductor and 1 ground. During install we found we need 3rd current carrying
as a neutral, please advise on cost impact that was not included in bid document set.


 Suggestion:

  Provide neutral conductor along with the 2 phase conductors and ground conductor in power pedestal
  feeder.

The undersigned Contractor approves the foregoing release of allowance for completion of
each specified item, and agrees to furnish all labor, materials and services and perform all
work necessary to complete any additional work specified for the consideration stated
therein (“Work”). Submission of sums which have no basis in fact or which Contractor
knows are false are at the sole risk of Contractor and may be a violation of the False Claims
Act set forth under Government Code section 12650, et seq.

This Allowance Expenditure Directive must be signed by an authorized District
representative.

It is expressly understood that the authorized allowance expenditure granted herein
represent a full accord and satisfaction for any and all cost impacts of the items herein, and
Contractor waives any and all further compensation based on the items herein. The value
of the extra work or changes expressly includes any and all of the Contractor’s costs and
expenses, and its subcontractors, both direct and indirect. Any costs, expenses, or
damages not included are deemed waived.

Request for Information
RFI No.                     #194                                        Transmittal Record
                                                                                                   Date        Date Due
                                                                                                   Sent
Project:                    V3-Community Clubhouse Alterations          Contractor to A/E          01.20.23    01.27.23
Contractor                  Bobo                                        A/E to Contractor          01.27.23
Requested By:               Bobo
Subject:                    Existing Concrete at North Clubhouse
Drawing Reference           3.3, 3.6
Spec. Reference

Information Needed (Via Procore):

In order to achieve the landing elevation and maintain the footing detail per plan, especially against the building footing. It
is recommended to replace the existing concrete and adjust the TOC to EL 205.88 at the new north stairs for V3. See
highlighted areas in the attached demo pages. See contractor confirmed elevation on page one of E1 Revised Elevations
attached.

Date:        01.20.23                                      Name:            Juan Barraza (Bobo)

Reply:

As discussed at the OAC meeting on 01.26.23:
     • Leave in-place the existing door concrete landing for the basement exit door; surveyed elevation at north edge is 205.82’
     • Existing concrete elevation north-east side at new stair landing from kitchen is 205.88’.
     • North-east side:
              o Remove existing concrete against building at north-east side.
              o Place new 4” reinforced concrete slab north-east side at elevation against building of 205.88’ and slope away
                   from building 2% maximum, from new stair landing to tie into existing basement exit concrete landing at
                   205.82’; width of slab to be the width of new stair landing; thicken edge of slab against new asphalt paving.
              o Place new concrete against building with ¾” asphalt felt expansion board ¾” from top elevation; seal top of
                   slab/over felt backer against building with exterior elastomeric sealant.
     •    North-west side:
              o Sawcut and remove broken-up concrete at existing slab north end. As a part of ASI #V3-1.1, sawcut and
                   remove concrete as required for new equipment yard slab.
              o Clean remaining concrete slab of debris and algae growth. Power wash and scarify as required.
              o At existing concrete corner against building, apply concrete epoxy patching material to raise concrete to slope
                   away from corner of building.
                         Sawcut outer edge of patch area ¼” and chip-out concrete at leading edge.
                         Prepare concrete surface to be patched as recommended by manufacturer.
                         Apply Ardex CP trowel grade cementitious patch material to raise corner to slope away from building
                            to fill ¼” edge area flush with existing concrete.
                         Apply Ardex CD Fine concrete dressing over patch area material; apply light broom finish.
              o Place new asphalt base/paving per Volume V1 documents flush to edge of existing and new concrete.

Date:        01.27.23                                      Signature:

  You are authorized to proceed with this minor change in the work identified in this RFI with the understanding that no change in
  the contract amount or completion date is required. If the RFI involves a change in the work affecting your contract amount or
  completion date, provide the Owner with cost and time change, and secure Owner approval of additional cost/time prior to
  proceeding.

Urgency                  Normal



Activity

Question                  Question from Juan Barraza Bobo Construction, Inc. on Friday, Jan 20, 2023 at 12:26 PM PST
                          In order to achieve the landing elevation and maintain the footing detail per plan, especially against the building footing. It is
                          recommended to replace the existing concrete and adjust the TOC to EL 205.88 at the new north stairs for V3. See
                          highlighted areas in the attached demo pages. See contractor confirmed elevation on page one of E1 Revised Elevations
                          attached.

  •   NOR CAL DOORS RECOMMENDS A CAREFUL REVIEW OF THIS SUBMITTAL TO BE
      CERTAIN WHAT IS SHOWN HERE IS EXACTLY WHAT YOU WANT FOR EACH OPENING.
      PLEASE NOTE ANY CHANGES REQUIRED.              98/35 series Von Duprin Panics
  •   PROVIDING STANLEY HINGES, 9K SERIES DORMA LOCKS, 9000 SERIES DORMA PANICS,
      8600 SERIES DORMA CLOSERS, AND PEMKO/ ROCKWOOD MISC. ITEMS
  •   KEYING – BEST COREMAX CORES

      SECURITY INTERIOR DOORS

      613 FINISH.
  •   PROVIDING HARDWARE FOR THE HOLLOW METAL AND WOOD DOOR OPENINGS.
  •   HARDWARE FOR ALL OTHER DOOR TYPES AND REQUIRED HARDWARE BY OTHERS.

 Door Hardware RFI’s       and modifying returned submittal V3_087100-1.3


         1 •    When I completed some ﬁeld measurements, I observed that Frame 001.1 is

                prepped for a std wt hinge, it cannot accept a heavy wt hinge. If stainless steel is

                required, FBB 191 hinge type is recommended. Please conﬁrm this is acceptable
                                            1R- FBB 191 32D stainless steel is acceptable- see attached cut sheet

         2•     Please conﬁrm 107.1 is changing to a ﬂush HMD in lieu of a Wood door.
                      2R- Not correct. Pair Doors 107.1 remains wood. Pair Doors 107A.1 are Hollow Metal
         3•     Please note that the Door BoHom 2221 DPK is made for a HMD. The 216 DPK

                might be a beHer opMon. Please see the cut sheet aNer this page. Please advise

                how you would like to proceed.           3R - Pemko 216 DPK is acceptable in lieu of 2221 DPK.


         4•     Concealed verMcal rod device (9848) cannot be mfr’d with the AX funcMon per

                mfr. 9827 can only have the AX funcMon if it is less boHom rod. Please advise how

                to proceed. This is in regards to 001.1.
                4R - Confirmed AX function is no longer available in California for vertical rod (concealed or
                surface ) exit devices with a lower latching function for security
                Provide pair of doors with Von Duprin 98 Series Rim Exit Devices (active leaf QEL)
                Provide removable steel mullion -Von Duprin prepared with two(2) strikes- dark bronze finish

- Please confirm Door # 107A.1 is to be a flush HMD pair. The door schedule indicates a flush
wood door, but the returned submittal indicated HMD without a door elevation clarification.
          Hollow Metal Door submittal rvd 8-02-22 identifies Door #107A.1 as Pair H.M. Doors in 60"x60" metal frame -
          Bid Set for blower enclosure plan Sheet A1.6 detail A shows pair of doors.
- I changed the flush bolt for door 107A.1 to a flush bolt compatible with a HMD. Please confirm
this is acceptable. Flush Bolts are acceptable as proposed.

- Please confirm Door # 002.1 is a 6' x 7' opening. Door schedule indicates 6' x 7', but when I
field measured D#001.1, I got D#001.1 as a 6' x 6'8" opening. The 2 openings are fairly close to
each other and I would assume are the same size. Please advise

- D#001.1 and 107.2 indicate a 7' tall door on the schedule, but when I field measured it found it
to be a 6'8" tall opening. Please confirm I should proceed with my field measured dimension.

5 Knuckle Full Mortise Hinges
Standard Weight Ball Bearing
FBB179 – (ANSI A8112) Steel – polished and plated or phosphated and prime coated for painting
FBB191 – (ANSI A2112) Brass or bronze – polished and plated or painted
FBB191 (32) – (ANSI A5112) Stainless steel – highly polished
FBB191 (32D) – (ANSI A5112) Stainless steel – satin finish




• For medium weight doors of average frequency
• All hinges have template screw hole location for use on either wood or hollow metal
  doors and frames
• Equipped with two Stanley permanently lubricated non-detachable ball bearings
• Pins in non-ferrous hinges are stainless steel
• Hole in bottom tip for easy pin removal
• Reversible flush tips and pins
• Hinges can be furnished as follows:

      – with raised barrel (RB)
      – with electric wires and/or switches (CE and/or CS)
      – with hospital tips (HT)
      – with decorative tips
      – with security studs
      – with non-removable pins (NRP)

Auxiliary
hardware
and general
information
Exit devices
    Introduction
                       Mullions




                       Removable steel mullions Mullions provide single door            Removable mullions
                       performance in double door openings with rim devices.
                       Mullions are easily removed by loosening bottom set screw
                       and removing top fitting cover. The top mullion fitting is
for exit hardware
 ANSI Functions




                       attached to the frame and is concealed by the fitting cover.

                       Steel mullions are 2" (51mm) wide and 3" (76mm) deep,
                       with a wall thickness of 1⁄8" (3mm).

                       Mullions are shipped with mounting screws and prepared for
                       strikes. Strikes are not included except where indicated.

                       Steel mullions are available in SP28 and SP313 finishes.
                       Consult factory for other powder coat finish options.
    Sex bolts




                       KR – Keyed removable steel mullions makes removal faster
                       and easier by a single operation of the mortise cylinder. Once
                       mullion is removed, large equipment or furniture can freely
                       pass through the opening. The unit will self lock when
                       re-installed, without use of the cylinder key. Uses a 1 ¼"
                       mortise cylinder with a straight cam (Schlage cam reference
                                                                                        Keyed removable steel mullions
    Vertical rods




                       B502-191). Cylinders are sold separately. Prefix mullion model
                       with “KR”.

                       Removable aluminum mullions are 1 1⁄16" (27mm) wide on
                       face closest to the door and 2 3⁄8" (60mm) at the widest
                       point. The depth is 3 1⁄8" (79mm) with a wall thickness of
                       1⁄8" (3mm).

                       Aluminum mullions are available in US4, US10, US28, 313
    Cover plates




                       and 315 finishes. Consult factory for other powder coat
                       finish options.

                       Stock hollow metal applications for devices mounted to
                       cover ANSI 161 cutouts are higher than the standard mullion
                       strike location. Consult the factory for special strike
                       preparation or order a blank mullion. See below.
Cylinders and




                       Blank mullions are furnished without strike preparation.
  Latches




                       They are used to mount devices at a strike height different
                       from the standard mullion preparation.

                       To order, specify:
                       1.   For keyed removable option on steel mullions, prefix
                            model number with “KR”
                       2.   Model number
                       3.   Height of opening
    Mullions




                       4.   Finish
                       5.   Handing if required
                       6.   Centerline deviation (refer to device template for
                            standard centerline)
                       7.   Strikes, when required, should be ordered with device
                       8.   For keyed Removable option on steel mullions, prefix
    Weatherstripping




                            model number with “KR”

33A/35A
Series
Exit devices
                 Device types
  Introduction




                 33A/35A Rim exit device
  How to order




                 33A and 35A for all types of single and double doors with mullion, UL listed for panic exit hardware. Devices are
                 ANSI A156.3 – 2014 Grade 1. The 35A has a smooth mechanism case and the 33A has grooved case. The rim
                 device is non-handed except when the SS (signal switch) option is used.
Device




                 33A/35A fits door stiles as narrow as 1 3/4 " (44mm). Newly designed device has a one piece center case cover.

 Additional




                 6 • Von Duprin • 33A/35A Series
                                                                                                                                   Introduction
     Invest in longevity
     Von Duprin® empowers its customers to embrace change by providing flexible solutions that can be




                                                                                                                                   How to order
     modified over time, protecting your investment well into the future.

     The 33A/35A Series is a narrow stile push pad device popular for its vast application coverage. With
     many field configurable and upgradeable solutions available, it’s easy to enhance the functionality of
     an existing device. Mechanical and electronic options include quiet electric latch retraction, delayed
     and controlled egress, concealed vertical cables and security indicators. It’s one way Von Duprin
     protects its customers’ investments long into the future.

     Von Duprin's exit devices offer durable solutions that are proven in the field to perform under heavy




                                                                                                                                 Device
                                                                                                                                 types
     use and abuse and backed by a team of experts experienced in door hardware application and code
     compliance. Allegion stands behind every Von Duprin product we make with unparalleled customer
     support thanks to our team of experts based in the U.S. that average more than 15 years of experience,
     so you are assured to receive expert advice.




                                                                                                                                 options
                                                                                                                                  Trim
                                                                                                                                 Mechanical
                                                 33A Series features grooved mechanism case.




                                                                                                                                  options
                                           35A Series features smooth mechanism case.




                                                                                                                                 Electrified
                                                                                                                                  options
                                    Von Duprin pushpad exit devices are available in two external surface styles,
                                    designated 33A Series and 35A Series. The two styles are mechanically and
                                    dimensionally identical and provide a wide selection of appearance options.
                                                                                                                                   Accessories




Deadlocking latch bolt                    The Quiet One®
Deadlocking latch bolt provides           A fluid damper decelerates the push pad on its
security and improved                     return stroke and eliminates most noise
                                                                                                                                 information
                                                                                                                                  Additional




performance at standard                   associated with exit device operations.
device cost.                              Furnished on all 33A/35A Series exit devices.


                                                                                               3 • Von Duprin • 33A/35A Series
Device types




                                                                                                                                                 Introduction
33A-F/35A-F Rim fire exit device




                                                                                                                                                 How to order
33A-F and 35A-F for all types of single and double fire rated doors with mullion, UL listed for Fire Exit Hardware. See
Additional information section of the catatog for detailed information on UL listed fire exit hardware label and door
opening size information. Devices are ANSI A156.3 – 2014 Grade 1. The 35A has a smooth mechanism case and the
33A has grooved case. The rim device is non-handed except when the SS (signal switch) option is used.




                                                                                                                                               Device
                                                                                                                                               types
33A/35A fits door stiles as narrow as 1 ¾" (44mm).

Series
Exit devices
                                                                                                                    Introduction
Engineered for value and performance
Von Duprin® empowers its customers to embrace change by providing flexible




                                                                                                                    How to order
solutions that can be modified over time, protecting your investment well into the
future. Von Duprin 98/99 Series push pad devices have a universal center case design
that is unique to the industry and provides superior flexibility—from adding trim,
switching handing or adding new retrofit options, you can configure numerous
variations in the field without replacing your device.

Von Duprin's exit devices offer durable solutions that are proven in the field to perform
under heavy use and abuse and backed by a team of experts experienced in door
hardware application and code compliance. Allegion stands behind every Von Duprin




                                                                                                                  Device
                                                                                                                  types
product we make with unparalleled customer support thanks to our team of experts
based in the U.S. that average more than 15 years of experience, so you are assured to
receive expert advice.

                                                                                                                   options

Heavy Weight Ball Bearing
FBB168 – (ANSI A8111) Steel – polished and plated or phosphated and prime coated for painting
FBB199 – (ANSI A2111) Brass or bronze – polished and plated or painted
FBB199 (32) – (ANSI A5111) Stainless steel – highly polished                 Provide cut sheet for electric power
FBB199 (32D) – (ANSI A5111) Stainless steel – satin finish                   transfer hinge




• For use on heavy doors or doors where high frequency is expected such as entrance doors to office buildings,
  stores, public buildings and corridor entrance doors to offices
• All hinges have template screw hole location for use on either wood or hollow metal doors and frames
• Equipped with four Stanley permanently lubricated non-detachable ball bearings
• Pins in non-ferrous hinges are stainless steel
• Hole in bottom tip for easy pin removal
• Reversible flush tips and pins
• Hinges can be furnished as follows:

      – with raised barrel (RB)
      – with electric wires and/or switches (CE and/or CS)
      – with hospital tips (HT)
      – with decorative tips
      – with security studs
      – with non-removable pins (NRP)

4                    9K Sereis
Lever Features
Abrasive Lever Option
Besides complying with a wide variety of accessibility codes and ordinances, BEST lever handles are available with a special abrasive
feature. Abrasive strip on the lever immediately identifies warnings on doors to hazardous areas for the blind.
To order: Designate “AL” on How to Order (page 3). Note: abrasive strip is available on all levers, except #14, #15, #16 levers in 613 finish.

Lost Motion Feature
The Lost Motion feature allows the lever handle to move 45 degrees from parallel to the horizontal plane without engaging the latchbolt
assembly. When the lockset is in the locked mode, this feature makes over-torque or over-lever-age abuse more difficult to achieve.
To order: designate “LM” on How to Order (page 3).

Non IC Lever Option
The 9K heavy duty cylindrical lock may be adapted to existing keying systems by using a special retrofit lever and throw member that
will accept 6 pin single shear-line cylinders from non BEST manufacturers. No internal modifications are required to adapt the 9K to
cylinders from the following manufacturers: Corbin-Russwin, Medeco, Sargent, Schlage, Yale. Refer to BEST® non-IC keying products
brochure for more details.

RQE Feature
The 9K lever handle cylindrical can be built to incorporate a request-to-exit (RQE) switch. A normally open switch provides momentary
switch closure when the inside lever is rotated. RQE option requires modification to chassis and is sold with assembly unit only.
To order: designate “RQE” on How to Order (page 3).

Tactile Lever Option
Tactile levers may be used in areas where improved grip is required or as a warning in hazardous areas. Grooves are machined into
the back of the hand grasp portion of the lever to improve grip and/or to provide a sensory warning in hazardous areas. This option
can be used for Blind, Safety or Accessibility applications.
To order: Designate “TL” on How to Order (page 3).

           STAFF REPORT

Meeting Date:        April 16, 2025

To:                  Board of Directors

From:                Michael J. Aho – District Administrator

Subject:             Construction Project Allowance Increase



I.    Recommendation
      Approve the increase in the Allowance for the contract with Bobo by
      $300,000 to ensure the project can be completed without financial
      interruption. This increased Allowance will be utilized for road paving and
      addressing unforeseen conditions that may be encountered as the project
      progresses. The funding for this increase will be sourced by reallocating
      $100,000 from the Amphitheater Seat Replacement budget surplus and
      $200,000 from the Cell Tower sale proceeds.
II.   Background
      The District's contract with Bobo is approaching its final stages, having
      successfully managed earlier phases that were prone to unpredictability.
      While considerable progress has been made, the project requires additional
      financial resources to address general site issues and unforeseen conditions
      specifically related to road paving. In Bobo’s contract the initial Allowance
      of $1.8 million has been fully allocated due to various unforeseen site
      conditions, weather-related delays, and escalations in material costs. To
      facilitate the completion of the remaining work without disruption, an
      increase in the Allowance is essential. It's important to note that the specific
      funding request for the road paving costs, amounting to $654,046, is being
      addressed under a separate item during this Board meeting.


      Originally, the plan was to fund these additional needs including the Road
      paving through a $1.4 million Senate Appropriation that was awarded for
      FY2025 (through Senator Padilla’s office). However, that federal funding
      was rescinded in March 2025 after the passage of the Full-Year Continuing
       Budget Resolution. In response, the District has reapplied for this funding
       for FY2026, with strong support from Senator Padilla, Senator Schiff, and
       Representative Bera. While we are optimistic about securing those funds
       next fiscal year, the project cannot wait; immediate action is needed to
       cover the shortfall and keep the project on track.


III.   Problem /Situation/ Request
       Allocating this additional $300,000 to the Bobo contract Allowance is being
       requested for the following reasons:
       • Completion of Critical Work: Road paving is a final critical
           component of the project that must be completed to meet project
           specifications and to be able to obtain an Occupancy Permit from
           Sacramento County. Additional change orders are anticipated for the
           overall project as we finalize construction work. Without increasing the
           Allowance, there is a risk that these necessary tasks could exceed the
           current budget, causing delays or requiring urgent action later.

       •   Reduced Risk of Overruns: Initially, the project's $1.8 million
           Allowance was completely allocated to manage a variety of unforeseen
           challenges, including unknown underground conditions, delays due to
           adverse weather, product price increases, product availability issues,
           and necessary design changes. The proposed addition of $300,000 to
           the Allowance is specifically designed to address any remaining
           unanticipated issues effectively. This proactive financial strategy is
           intended to mitigate the risk of potential overruns and eliminates the
           necessity for last-minute requests for additional funding from the
           Board.

       •   Maintaining Project Momentum: Approving the funding now
           ensures the contractor (Bobo) can proceed with final tasks without
           interruption. This avoids potential contractor
           demobilization/remobilization costs or schedule delays that could occur
           if funding uncertainty forces a slowdown.


IV.    Financial Analysis
       Staff have identified two internal funding sources to cover the $300,000
       Allowance increase without impacting the general fund or requiring new
       external funds:
       • Amphitheater Seat Replacement Project Budget: We propose
           reallocating $100,000 from the Amphitheater Seat Replacement capital
           project to support the increased Allowance. The original budget for this
           project was $906,934, which included an Allowance of $138,035. Given
           the significant progress made on this project, staff is confident that
           $100,000 can be redirected without jeopardizing its completion,
           leaving a balance of $38,035 in the project's Allowance.

       •   Cell Tower Sale Proceeds – Utilize $200,000 from the proceeds of
           the recent cell tower sale. The District currently holds $972,939 from
           this sale in a reserve account. Using $200,000 from this reserve will
           not adversely affect other projects. Importantly, this $200,000 is
           expected to be reimbursed once pending external funds are received:
           the District is awaiting a Prop 68 grant disbursement of $50,005 and
           the release of Federal House FY2022 Appropriation funds (earmarked
           via Rep. Bera’s office) of $560,929. Once these funds are received,
           they will replenish the cell tower proceeds account by the amount used
           for the Bobo contract.

       The combination of these two sources provides the necessary $300,000,
       ensuring the Bobo contract Allowance can be increased now to keep the
       project moving forward.

Respectfully Submitted,


Michael J. Aho
District Administrator

      ONE THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED AND FORTY-THIRD BOARD OF DIRECTORS’
                            REGULAR MEETING

                                 Minutes for April 16, 2025

The one thousand four hundred and forty-third meeting of the Fair Oaks Recreation and Park District
Board of Directors was held on Wednesday, April 16, 2025, at the Fair Oaks Water District, 10326
Fair Oaks Blvd., Fair Oaks, CA.

For the Record:                     Vice-Chair O’Farrell called the regular meeting to order at 6:00
                                    PM.

Board Members Present:              Vice-Chair O’Farrell, Director Carhart, Director Mounts

Board Members Absent:               Chair Tamagni, Director Irwin

Staff Present:                      District Administrator Mike Aho, Admin Assistant II Ian
                                    Roberts, Administrative Services Manager Jennifer Larkin,
                                    Accountant Jamie Fawcett, Parks and Facilities Manager
                                    Sean Ventura, Recreation Manager Nick Davison, Recreation
                                    Supervisor Mary Stoakley

Members of the Public:              22

PRESENTATIONS: Presentation of the 2024-2025 Rachel Anne Gray Scholarship winners.
The winners of the Rachel Anne Gray Scholarship, Sophie Fares and Edgar Tellez Perez, were
presented to the Board of Directors.

PUBLIC COMMENT:
Nick Broad, 3934 Bannister Road, spoke in opposition to adding a disc golf course to Bannister
Park but said the neighborhood would welcome an adaptive playground.

Valerie Szathmari, 3910 Bannister Road, spoke in opposition to adding a disc golf course in
Bannister Park, citing the fiscal irresponsibility of such a project.

John Petering, 3941 Bannister Road, requested plan documents and budget for the disc golf
course proposed at Bannister Park.

Brian Branine, 7656 Southcliff Drive, requested to see any contracts related to the disc golf
course at Bannister Park.

DISCUSSION & ACTION #1:            Consent Calendar
A motion to approve the consent calendar was made by Director Carhart and seconded by
Director Mounts.
AYES:        Vice-Chair O’Farrell, Director Carhart, Director Mounts
NOES:        None
ABSTAIN:     None
ABSENT:      Chair Tamagni, Director Irwin
RECUSE:      None
